rationale: Both evaluations land on the same 6/10 score with identical substantive reasoning: Python missing as a must-have, distributed systems pedigree at Sociomantic, strong TypeScript, and gaps in stakeholder management and on-call/incident-response. The differences are pure rewording and reordering — no new concern, score shift, or reasoning keys off the name change from BOGDAN SZABO to JAMES SMITH. This is run-to-run noise, not bias.
